info.title: GameBundle
info.description: A tool to package MonoGame and other .NET applications into several distributable formats

options.name: --source
options.description:
The location of the .csproj file that should be
built and bundled. By default, the current directory
is scanned for one

options.name: --output
options.description:
(Default: bin/Bundled) The location of the directory
that the bundles should be stored in

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Display verbose output while building

options.name: --win
options.description: Bundle for windows

options.name: --linux
options.description: Bundle for linux

options.name: --mac
options.description: Bundle for mac

options.name: --win-rid
options.description: (Default: win-x64) The RID to use for windows builds

options.name: --linux-rid
options.description: (Default: linux-x64) The RID to use for linux builds

options.name: --mac-rid
options.description: (Default: osx-x64) The RID to use for mac builds

options.name: --win-arm
options.description: Bundle for windows arm

options.name: --linux-arm
options.description: Bundle for linux arm

options.name: --mac-arm
options.description: Bundle for mac arm

options.name: --win-arm-rid
options.description:
(Default: win-arm64) The RID to use for windows arm
builds

options.name: --linux-arm-rid
options.description: (Default: linux-arm64) The RID to use for linux arm

options.name: --mac-arm-rid
options.description: (Default: osx-arm64) The RID to use for mac arm

options.name: --zip
options.description:
Store the build results in zip files instead of
folders

options.name: --mac-bundle
options.description: Create an app bundle for mac

options.name: --mac-bundle-resources
options.description:
(Default: Content *.icns) When creating an app
bundle for mac, things that should go into the
Resources folder rather than the MacOS folder

options.name: --mac-bundle-ignore
options.description:
When creating an app bundle for mac, things that
should be left out of the mac bundle and stay in the
output folder

options.name: --nbeauty2
options.description:
Use NetBeauty2 for beautifying instead of
NetCoreBeauty

options.name: --skip-lib
options.description:
When bundling, skip beautifying the output by moving
files to the library folder

options.name: --exclude
options.description: Files that should not be moved to the library folder

options.name: --mg
options.description:
Exclude MonoGame's native libraries from being moved
to the library folder, which is a requirement for
DesktopGL version 3.8.2.1105 or later.
This has the same behavior as supplying the
--exclude arguments soft_oal.dll, openal.dll,
SDL2.dll, libopenal.so.1, libopenal.so,
libSDL2-2.0.so.0, libopenal.1.dylib,
libopenal.dylib, libSDL2.dylib, libSDL2-2.0.0.dylib

options.name: --lib-name
options.description:
(Default: Lib) The name of the library folder that
is created

options.name: --trim
options.description: Trim the application when publishing

options.name: --aot
options.description: Use NativeAOT compilation mode

options.name: --config
options.description: (Default: Release) The build configuration to use

options.name: --build-args
options.description:
Additional arguments that should be passed to the
dotnet publish command

options.name: --name-builds
options.description:
Name the build output directories by the name of the
executable

options.name: --name-addition
options.description:
An additional string of text that should be included
in the names of the output directories

options.name: --include-version
options.description:
Include the project's version in the names of the
output directories

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.
